title: 越南6月推5G服務， 向「華為」技術說不

replies.msg:
https://ec.ltn.com.tw/article/breakingnews/3046021

有骨氣！越南6月推5G服務 向華為技術說不
2020-01-20 17:51

隸屬於越南國防部的越南最大行動通訊業者「越南軍用電子電信公司」（Viettel），今年稍後將推出商用5G服務，其所採用的是自行研發的技術，而非中國電信大廠華為。（美聯社）

〔編譯盧永山／綜合報導〕隸屬於越南國防部的越南最大行動通訊業者「越南軍用電子電信公司」（Viettel），今年稍後將推出商用5G服務，其所採用的是自行研發的技術，而非中國電信大廠華為的，這也凸顯兩國的關係緊張。

根據英國金融時報報導，Viettel是在上週五（1月17日）用國內製造的設備，進行首度影音通話連結測試後，宣布自6月起將開始部署5G網路，明年完成全國部署。該公司表示，其研發部門Viettel High Technology花了6個月時間開發了上述影音通話所需的軟硬體。

全球沒有多少企業成功開發出商用5G技術。Viettel表示，該公司是繼愛立信、諾基亞、華為、三星電子、中興通訊之後的第6家，且在柬埔寨、海地、祕魯等全球11國擁有逾1.1億名客戶。

越南共產黨領袖屢屢對中國在南中國海的軍事行動表達不滿，因而在立場上親近美國。美國則以國家安全為由，遊說各國不要採用華為的5G設備，越南成為附和美國行動的東南亞首國。

澳洲新南威爾斯大學榮譽教授塞爾（Carl Thayer）表示：「5G技術是最新戰線，Viettel想要並駕齊驅，開發自有技術。此外，越南也擔心遭駭客攻擊，同時美國也對越南施加壓力。」

另一方面， 英國政府官員再發炮指不滿美國政府， 因為美國政府正迫使英國政府禁止「華為」參與英國5G網絡"非核心"建設。

https://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-7903489/amp/Trump-envoy-heated-No10-row-Huawei-5G-deal.html?__twitter_impression=true

By Harry Cole and Abul Taher for The Mail on Sunday
00:36 19 Jan 2020, updated 01:09 19 Jan 2020
。。
But Mr Johnson has also been told that his manifesto commitment for superfast broadband for all by 2025 will be in tatters without Huawei, because no other commercial company has its technical advantage. A Whitehall source said: ‘It’s Huawei or the highway before 2025.’

Yesterday Government officials hit back at the US pressure, saying ‘we are very unimpressed by what they have been doing’.

A senior Government source accused the US of ‘repeatedly telling us what not to do but have no US solution to offer from Silicon Valley or anywhere else. We’ve asked on multiple occasions and all that’s come back is silence or shake of the head.’

The Mail on Sunday understands that the US Government is poised to suggest an alternative Western technological solution to avoid allies having to rely on Huawei, but the plan would take many years to become operational.

By Harry Cole and Abul Taher for The Mail on Sunday
00:36 19 Jan 2020, updated 01:09 19 Jan 2020

But Mr Johnson has also been told that his manifesto commitment for superfast broadband for all by 2025 will be in tatters without Huawei, because no other commercial company has its technical advantage. A Whitehall source said: ‘It’s Huawei or the highway before 2025.’

Yesterday Government officials hit back at the US pressure, saying ‘we are very unimpressed by what they have been doing’.

A senior Government source accused the US of ‘repeatedly telling us what not to do but have no US solution to offer from Silicon Valley or anywhere else. We’ve asked on multiple occasions and all that’s come back is silence or shake of the head.’

The Mail on Sunday understands that the US Government is poised to suggest an alternative Western technological solution to avoid allies having to rely on Huawei, but the plan would take many years to become operational.
